data.title: NBIS, AFRM, CHWY: 3 High P/E Stocks Ready to Outperform with 30% Upside
data.description:
Using the TipRanks Stock Screener Tool, we identified three large-cap companies that stand out with high price-to-earnings (P/E) ratios, a Strong Buy consensus rating, and an impressive upside potential of more than 30% over the next twelve months, making them compelling choices for investors.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium and Invest with Confidence

  • Unlock hedge-fund level data and powerful investing tools designed to help you make smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis so your portfolio is always positioned for maximum potential

An investment's true value, whether in stocks or real estate, rests on its expected future growth. High P/E ratios often signal an "expensive" stock, but they do not reveal the full picture. Real insight emerges when you compare the current price to projected future earnings growth.

Buying high P/E stocks means betting on explosive future growth, despite sky-high current valuations. Success hinges on picking companies with rock-solid fundamentals, relentless innovation, and widening markets, not just any lofty multiple.

Here Are This Week's High P/E Stocks

Nebius Group (NBIS) – Nebius has a P/E ratio of 102x, significantly higher than the sector average of about 32x. On TipRanks, the average Nebius price target of $164.20 implies 74% upside potential from current levels. Meanwhile, NBIS stock has rocketed over 240% year-to-date.

Nebius runs a full-stack AI cloud platform with Nvidia (NVDA) GPU (graphics processing unit) clusters for training and deploying AI models globally. The company offers efficient infrastructure for AI in healthcare, robotics, and finance, and is set to benefit from the booming AI demand, long-term contracts, cost edges, and strong growth potential.

Affirm Holdings (AFRM) – The buy now, pay later (BNPL) giant has a P/E ratio of 105x, about 600% higher than the sector average. On TipRanks, the average Affirm price target of $96.11 implies 39.2% upside potential. Meanwhile, AFRM shares have gained nearly 13.4% year-to-date.

Affirm has shown strong revenue growth in fiscal 2025 and a recent move into profitability after prior losses. It has repeatedly beaten earnings expectations and is scaling products like the Affirm Card on top of a large user and merchant base, which could support continued growth if BNPL adoption remains strong.

Chewy (CHWY) – Chewy has a P/E ratio of 99.8x, 250% higher than its sector average of 20.3x. On TipRanks, the average Chewy price target of $46.86 implies 37% upside potential. Year-to-date, CHWY shares have gained 2%.

Chewy is a leading online pet retailer, showcasing a robust growth profile, fueled by steady growth, market share gains, and reliable recurring revenue from the Autoship subscription model. Earnings also consistently surpass expectations, supported by a growing active customer base and profitability improvements amid resilient pet spending.

To find more stocks like these, explore TipRanks' Stock Screener Tool, which provides an updated list of stocks that can be filtered and scanned using various parameters.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: 5 High-Yield Dividend Stocks Set to Dominate the Market in the New Year
data.description:
As the market looks ahead to a year potentially marked by an improving economy and lower interest rates, high-yield dividend stocks are positioned to become an increasingly attractive option for investors in 2026.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium and Invest with Confidence

  • Unlock hedge-fund level data and powerful investing tools designed to help you make smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis so your portfolio is always positioned for maximum potential

While dividend growth stocks may have lagged the broader market in 2025, analysts are optimistic about the upcoming year. The overall economic outlook is solid, and more rate cuts by the Federal Reserve are expected to make high dividend yields more appealing compared to fixed-income alternatives like Treasury bills.

Importantly, the S&P 500's (SPX) dividend payout ratio sits near a historical low of 30%, suggesting that corporations have significant headroom to boost their payouts.

Here are five high-yield dividend stocks to consider for your 2026 portfolio, balancing strong current yields with growth potential:

1. Exxon Mobil (XOM)

  • Sector: Energy
  • Approximate Yield: 3.35%
  • Case for 2026: Exxon Mobil (XOM) offers one of the market's most impressive dividend safety records, having increased its payout for 43 consecutive years, classifying it as a Dividend Aristocrat contender. Analysts are favorable toward the energy sector as a "bond proxy" due to its relatively high yields. With the strong profit cycle expected to continue into 2026, Exxon's position as a heavyweight in the integrated oil sector, combined with a respectable yield, makes it a core high-yield holding.

2. Chevron (CVX)

  • Sector: Energy
  • Approximate Yield: 4.52%
  • Case for 2026: Offering the highest yield among this group and having raised its dividend for 38 consecutive years, Chevron (CVX) is another top pick within the energy sector. The stock benefits from the same tailwinds as Exxon, which is a strong profit cycle and the general appeal of high-yield sectors as rates soften. Its large yield and decades-long history of dividend increases make it highly attractive for income-focused investors looking for stability in the energy space.

3. AbbVie (ABBV)

  • Sector: Healthcare
  • Approximate Yield: 2.91%
  • Case for 2026: The pharmaceutical giant AbbVie (ABBV) is a core holding in high-dividend-yield exchange-traded funds (ETFs). Despite its relatively short history as a standalone company, AbbVie has demonstrated a commitment to returning capital to shareholders, showing 11 consecutive years of dividend increases. Its yield of over 3% positions it well, and the healthcare sector provides a defensive element in an uncertain economic environment.

4. Coca-Cola (KO)

  • Sector: Consumer Staples
  • Approximate Yield: 2.95%
  • Case for 2026: Coca-Cola (KO) is a blue-chip Dividend Aristocrat, having increased its dividend for over 50 straight years. While its yield is slightly lower than the energy giants, it represents a premium play on dividend growth and stability. Chris Senyek of Wolfe Research suggests that the market in 2026 may favor "faster-growing companies, and that tends to be more in dividend growth," making Aristocrats like Coca-Cola a key strategic inclusion. The consumer staples sector also provides resilience regardless of economic cycles.

5. FedEx (FDX)

  • Sector: Industrials/Air Freight & Logistics
  • Approximate Yield: 1.99%
  • Case for 2026: FedEx (FDX) stands out as a strong dividend growth candidate. While its current yield is the lowest on this list, it boasts a low payout ratio of approximately 32.63% (suggesting plenty of room for future hikes) and a compelling track record of high dividend growth rates over the past decade. It fits the preferred long-term strategy of buying companies with high dividend growth and high cash-flow yields, as a continually improving economy in 2026 should fuel its earnings and support further robust dividend increases.

Key Takeaway for Investors

As market volatility continues and the potential for Fed rate cuts looms, a strategy that combines high-yield names (like the energy stocks) with consistent dividend growth champions (like Coca-Cola and FedEx) appears prudent for 2026. As one analyst summarizes the environment: "It's a confluence of different factors that make dividend strategies, in general, more attractive than they've been over the past couple of years."

Investors can compare all five dividend stocks side-by-side on the TipRanks Stocks Comparison Tool. Click on the image below to find out more.
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: Here's Why CbdMD Stock (YCBD) Rocketed Today
data.description:
CbdMD (YCBD) stock surged on Friday despite a lack of news from the cannabis company. The latest press release and filing from the company were on Monday, when investors were notified that it had regained compliance with listing standards. However, that doesn't appear to be what has the stock up today.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium and Invest with Confidence

  • Unlock hedge-fund level data and powerful investing tools designed to help you make smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis so your portfolio is always positioned for maximum potential

Instead, news from Washington is likely behind the rally of YCBD stock today. President Donald Trump is reportedly set to direct regulators to change marijuana to a Schedule III drug. This is a less severe restriction that is shared with many common prescription painkillers. Insiders claim the President would use an executive order to change the regulations around marijuana.

Investors note that this move won't legalize or decriminalize marijuana. Even so, businesses that deal in the drug would have an easier time selling it, and it would also open the way to new research into the effects of cannabis. Considering cbdMD's role as a CBD distributor, it makes sense that the company's shares would rise alongside these reports.

CbdMD Stock Movement Today

CbdMD stock was up 96.51% in pre-market trading on Friday, following a slight dip yesterday. The stock has also fallen 78.97% year-to-date and 79.81% over the past 12 months.

With today's news came heavy trading of YCBD stock, as more than 68 million shares changed hands. To put that in perspective, the company's three-month daily average trading volume is about 7.54 million units.
data.author: William White

data.title: Stock Futures Trade Mixed as Investors Shift to Value Stocks
data.description:
U.S. stock futures traded mixed on Friday morning, after investors continued to shift to value stocks from artificial intelligence (AI) plays in reaction to Oracle's (ORCL) fiscal second-quarter results. The database software and cloud computing company missed revenue expectations and sparked concerns about elevated AI spending. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX) and the S&P 500 (SPX) were down 0.49% and 0.09%, respectively, while futures tied to the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) were up 0.21% at 8:21 a.m. EST on December 12.

TipRanks Cyber Monday Sale

  • Claim 60% off TipRanks Premium for data-backed insights and research tools you need to invest with confidence.
  • Subscribe to TipRanks' Smart Investor Picks and see our data in action through our high-performing model portfolio - now also 60% off

During Thursday's regular trading session, the Dow Jones and the S&P 500 rose 1.34% and 0.21%, respectively, reaching record highs. Meanwhile, the Nasdaq Composite fell 0.26% as investors moved away from high-flying technology stocks.

Turning to trending stocks, shares of chip company Broadcom (AVGO) were down 6.2% in Friday's pre-market trading despite reporting better-than-expected earnings for the fourth quarter of Fiscal 2025. Meanwhile, AI stocks, including Oracle, Micron Technology (MU), and CoreWeave (CRWV), continued to trend lower today, as of writing. Meanwhile, Lululemon (LULU) stock rallied 10% after the athleisure company announced better-than-expected Q3 results and its CEO's departure.

Also, Tilray (TLRY) stock rallied 29% while Canopy Growth (CGC) shares jumped 23% following reports that President Donald Trump will significantly ease federal restrictions on marijuana.

data.title: Nvidia Stock Has a Powerful Catalyst Coming. Analyst Says Blackwell Superclusters Will Be "a Full Generation Ahead of Competition"
data.description:
Nvidia (NVDA) stock has been stuck in a trading range for the last month, falling about 4.9% and heading for small weekly losses. Shareholders who are waiting for a change might have to wait a little longer, but a major event in early 2026 could be the powerful spark needed to drive the stock higher.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium and Invest with Confidence

  • Unlock hedge-fund level data and powerful investing tools designed to help you make smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis so your portfolio is always positioned for maximum potential

The main problems weighing on the stock are simple worries about the overall AI market and growing competition. Broadcom (AVGO), a key competitor, saw its stock drop even after good earnings because investors wanted more news about new AI chip customers. More importantly, Google's (GOOGL) Tensor Processing Units (TPUs), custom chips designed for AI, are seen as a rising threat to Nvidia's dominance.

Why Blackwell Is Nvidia's Catalyst

The biggest hope for shaking Nvidia out of its sideways trading is the expected rollout of new AI models. These cutting-edge models are being trained on the company's latest, most powerful hardware: the Nvidia Blackwell NVL72 superclusters.

Bank of America Securities analyst Vivek Arya believes these new, major model releases will be the key factor. "Blackwell-based models should begin to roll out starting early 2026, by the time when Nvidia believes it would be clear that they are at least a full generation ahead of competition," Arya wrote in a recent note.

The analyst gave Nvidia an "Overweight" rating and set a $275 price target on the stock, arguing that the release of these new Blackwell-based models could change the momentum and settle the debate against Google's TPUs.

Nvidia Aims for the Best GPU Architecture

Google's TPUs are custom chips designed specifically for AI training and inference. They are less flexible than Nvidia's GPUs but are highly efficient for Google's own AI work. The success of powerful new Google models, like Gemini 3, has shown the industry that non-Nvidia hardware can achieve top-tier results, causing worries about Nvidia's near-monopoly.

Therefore, the upcoming Blackwell-trained models are not just a normal product refresh; they are a necessary show of strength to prove that Nvidia remains a "full generation ahead" and that its powerful GPU architecture is still the best and most necessary hardware for the world's most advanced AI research.

Is Nvidia a Buy, Hold, or Sell?

Analyst sentiment for Nvidia (NVDA) is highly bullish, maintaining a consensus rating of Strong Buy. This is based on ratings from 41 Wall Street analysts in the last three months, with 39 analysts recommending a Buy, one recommending a Hold, and one recommending a Sell. The average 12-month consensus price target for NVDA is $258.45. This average target represents a substantial 42.9% upside from the last closing price.
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: Why Is Jiuzi Stock (JZXN) Up Today?
data.description:
Jiuzi (JZXN) stock rocketed higher on Friday after the new energy vehicle retail operator announced a Memorandum of Understanding (MOU) to increase its private placement size to up to $1 billion. This MOU was reached with several institutional investors and is an expansion of the $12 million private placement plan that was revealed on October 7, 2025.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium and Invest with Confidence

  • Unlock hedge-fund level data and powerful investing tools designed to help you make smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis so your portfolio is always positioned for maximum potential

Jiuzi said that the funds from this private placement will be used to expand its crypto asset business, including building advanced secure custody infrastructure and innovative storage solutions. The company noted that this will allow it to capitalize on the growing market demand for crypto asset services.

Tao Li, CEO of Jiuzi, said, "The strong trust placed by investors validates our strategic vision and execution capabilities. This substantial additional capital provides us with significant financial flexibility to build secure crypto asset custody infrastructure and pursue strategic acquisition opportunities within the rapidly evolving crypto asset storage ecosystem."

Jiuzi Stock Movement Today

Jiuzi stock was up 44.4% in pre-market trading on Friday, following a 6.04% dip yesterday. The stock has also decreased 89.9% year-to-date and 92.09% over the past 12 months.

With today's news came strong early-morning trading of JZXN stock, as more than 5.5 million shares changed hands. For comparison, the stock's three-month daily average trading volume is about 7 million units.

There's a lack of analyst coverage for Jiuzi, meaning traders will want to be careful about making an investment in the company. That's doubly true considering its volatile nature and the large drops experienced in 2025.
data.author: William White

data.title: HOOD, IBTA, IRDM: Cathie Wood Buys the Dip in Robinhood, Sells Ibotta and Iridium Stocks
data.description:
Cathie Wood's ARK Invest ETFs (exchange-traded funds) reported several key trades for Thursday, December 11. The prominent hedge fund manager capitalized on the 9% drop in Robinhood Markets (HOOD) shares, an online brokerage firm, due to weak November trading volumes. At the same time, the firm continued to trim positions across several innovation-focused names, including Ibotta (IBTA) and Iridium Communications (IRDM).

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium and Invest with Confidence

  • Unlock hedge-fund level data and powerful investing tools designed to help you make smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis so your portfolio is always positioned for maximum potential

Wood Increases Exposure in Robinhood Stock

ARK's largest move of the day was a strong buy in Robinhood. The firm bought 124,427 shares worth about $16.9 million through ARK Innovation ETF (ARKK) and the ARK Next Generation Internet ETF (ARKW). This follows a smaller buy on December 5 and signals rising confidence in Robinhood as user activity and new products gain traction.

Robinhood reported a sharp slowdown in November activity: equity trading volumes fell 37%, options dropped 28%, and crypto slipped 12%. Weaker markets also pushed assets under custody down 5% to $325 billion, marking the first decline since February. Even so, the firm still posted solid net deposits of $7.1 billion, up 27% from October. 

On TipRanks, Robinhood's stock has a consensus Moderate Buy rating among 20 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 14 Buy, five Hold, and one Sell recommendations issued in the last three months. The average HOOD price target of $151.39 implies 22.57% upside from current levels.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: NVDA Stock Forecast: Analysts Say Nvidia Could Hit $258, Up 43% from Current Levels
data.description:
Nvidia (NVDA) stock remains in the spotlight amid growing concerns over an artificial intelligence (AI) bubble. The chipmaker's shares have already surged nearly 35% in 2025, and Wall Street sees another 43% upside over the next twelve months. Nvidia bulls continue to dominate bears, fueled by major catalysts like the AI data center boom, new GPU (graphics processing unit) launches, strong earnings momentum, and market expansion. Yet some analysts argue the shares are fairly valued given this growth potential.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium and Invest with Confidence

  • Unlock hedge-fund level data and powerful investing tools designed to help you make smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis so your portfolio is always positioned for maximum potential

On TipRanks, NVDA stock commands a Strong Buy consensus rating based on 39 Buys, one Hold, and one Sell rating. Also, the average Nvidia price target of $258.45 implies 42.8% upside potential from current levels. Let's understand some analysts' views in detail.

Analysts Hail Nvidia as the AI King

BofA Securities' top analyst Vivek Arya has a Buy rating and $275 price target, implying 52% upside potential. He noted that NVDA GPUs lead competitors by a full generation. Blackwell offers 10-15x better model training, while Vera Rubin is slated for second half of 2026. Arya pointed to clear demand visibility for at least $500 billion in cumulative sales over 2025-26, with Google (GOOGL) as a key, growing customer and nearly every major model builder still relying on NVDA.

Similarly, Wells Fargo analyst Aaron Rakers reaffirmed his $265 price target, implying 46.5% upside potential after the White House lifted the ban on export of H200 chips to China. Rakers estimates this unlocks $25 to $30 billion in annual revenue and adds $0.60 to $0.70 to earnings per share.

Nvidia Faces Rising Chip Competition

In contrast, Seaport Global analyst Jay Goldberg reiterated his Sell rating and $140 price target, implying 22.6% downside potential. He believes Nvidia faces rising competition and is using various sales tactics to cope, which are not yet fully visible in financials but are already significant and expected to grow in 2026. He noted that Alphabet (GOOGL) has done a strong job getting outside customers to use its in-house TPUs (tensor processing units). He added that although TPUs are a niche fit, they can beat Nvidia systems on several performance measures.

Is NVDA Stock a Buy?

Wall Street rates NVDA a Strong Buy, with 43% upside from current levels.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth
